admin.users.erase.heading: Erase account (DPDP)
admin.users.erase.warning: This permanently deletes {user}'s contact details, sign-in codes, sessions, and consent record, and replaces their account with an anonymous placeholder. Their past votes, flags, and audit history stay on record but can never again be traced back to them. This cannot be undone.
admin.users.erase.confirmLabel: I understand this permanently and irreversibly erases {user}'s identity and cannot be undone.
admin.users.error.confirmRequired: Confirm you understand the effect of this action before continuing.
admin.users.error.userNotFound: No user found for that id.
admin.users.error.cannotBanSelf: You can't ban your own account. Ask another admin to do this.
admin.users.error.cannotBanLastAdmin: You can't ban the last admin. Grant another admin first.
admin.users.error.cannotEraseSelf: You can't erase your own account. Ask another admin to do this.
admin.users.error.cannotEraseLastAdmin: You can't erase the last admin. Grant another admin first.
admin.users.error.cannotBanErased: This account has already been erased and can't be banned.
admin.users.error.cannotReactivateErased: This account has been erased and can never be reactivated.

admin.partners.error.eoiAlreadyProcessed: This expression of interest has already been accepted or declined.
admin.audit.meta.title: Audit log — Bangalore Votes
admin.audit.meta.description: Every published change, who made it, from which source, and when — with a forward-write restore for candidate report-card fields.

admin.audit.filters.heading: Filters
admin.audit.list.heading: Entries
admin.audit.list.empty: No audit entries match these filters.
admin.audit.restore.confirmLabel: I understand this publishes the old value forward as a new change — it does not edit history.
admin.audit.notice.restored: Value restored — published forward as a new entry (see below).
admin.audit.error.notRestorable: This entry can't be restored — it doesn't record a directly-republishable field value.
admin.audit.error.entryNotFound: No audit entry found for that id.
partnerKit.meta.title: Partner kit — {name}
partnerKit.meta.description: Ready-to-forward links, WhatsApp messages, and a neutrality statement for {name}.
partnerKit.heading: Partner kit for {name}
partnerKit.intro: Everything you need to forward this platform to your own network — your tagged link, ready-to-paste WhatsApp messages in English and Kannada, a poster, and an answer ready if anyone asks whether this is campaigning.
partnerKit.notFound.fact: We couldn't find a partner kit at this link.
partnerKit.taggedLink.heading: Your link
partnerKit.taggedLink.helper: Share this exact link — it's tagged so we know it reached people through you.
partnerKit.forward.general.heading: General message
partnerKit.forward.general.body: Find your new GBA ward and see who's standing for corporator — a neutral, sourced guide for every Bengaluru ward, in English and Kannada: {link}
partnerKit.forward.firstTimeVoter.body: Voting for the first time? Here's a simple checklist to get ready — checking your registration, getting your voter ID sorted, and knowing what to expect on election day: {link}
partnerKit.poster.heading: Poster
partnerKit.poster.pendingNote: Poster image — asset pending. A WhatsApp-sized poster will appear here once it's ready.
partnerKit.neutrality.heading: If someone asks whether this is campaigning
partnerKit.neutrality.statement: This is a neutral, non-partisan civic information platform run by the Oorvani Foundation. Forwarding it is sharing voter information, not campaigning — it carries no candidate endorsement of any kind.

__hints.ward.candidates.meta.title: Browser tab title for the candidates-in-ward list page (/ward/{id}/candidates, IA §3.3); {wardName} is the ward's name in the page's own language.
__hints.ward.candidates.meta.description: Meta description for the candidates-in-ward list page.
__hints.ward.candidates.list.label: aria-label on the candidates-in-ward list (IA §3.3) — alphabetical order, never editorially ranked (design-system §4.3).
__hints.ward.candidates.links.label: aria-label on the candidates-in-ward page's nav to the Compare-candidates entry point.
__hints.ward.candidates.empty.awaited: EmptyState fact shown when a ward has no candidates yet and app_settings.notification_date is not set (design-system §7.12) — the ward exists and this is a 200, not a 404 (architecture §8).
__hints.ward.candidates.empty.fact: EmptyState fact shown when a ward has no candidates yet and app_settings.notification_date IS set; {date} is the raw stored date string (same convention as home.election.status), design-system §7.12.
__hints.candidate.status.filed: Lifecycle status label shown next to every candidate row in the candidates-in-ward list (IA §3.3) — plain neutral text, never a red/alarm chip (design-system §4).
__hints.candidate.status.contesting: See candidate.status.filed — same neutral-text convention for the 'contesting' lifecycle status.
__hints.candidate.status.rejected: See candidate.status.filed — same neutral-text convention for the 'rejected' lifecycle status; the candidate row itself is never hidden (PRD §5.2).
__hints.candidate.status.withdrawn: See candidate.status.filed — same neutral-text convention for the 'withdrawn' lifecycle status; the candidate row itself is never hidden (PRD §5.2).
__hints.candidate.provisionalMarker: Subtle marker shown on the candidate report card (and candidate lists elsewhere) until app_settings.withdrawals_closed is 'true' — PRD §5.2/§9.3: the filed list may still shrink via withdrawal.
__hints.candidate.statusBanner.withdrawn: Neutral NOTICE banner (not an error) shown on a withdrawn candidate's report card, at HTTP 200 — the URL was already shared and must keep resolving (PRD §5.2, architecture §8).
__hints.candidate.statusBanner.rejected: Neutral NOTICE banner (not an error) shown on a scrutiny-rejected candidate's report card, at HTTP 200 — same rationale as the withdrawn banner.
__hints.candidate.header.sourceEcNomination: Static provenance note under the candidate's name/photo/party header — these three fields have no candidate_fields row (their source is the EC nomination itself, not a curator/affidavit entry), so this is plain text, not one of SourceLine's three reserved provenance badges.
__hints.candidate.field.cases: Field-row label for criminal record / pending cases (EC affidavit-sourced). Renders in plain ink like every other field — no alarm/danger styling (design-system §4.4: the data speaks without alarm coloring).
__hints.compare.meta.title: Browser tab title for the candidate comparison page (/ward/{id}/compare, IA §3.5); {wardName} is the ward's name in the page's own language.
__hints.compare.meta.description: Meta description for the candidate comparison page.
__hints.compare.pageHeading: Subheading under the ward name on the comparison page — same page, no per-candidate ranking implied by this text.
__hints.compare.table.caption: Visually-hidden <caption> on the comparison <table> — names the table for screen-reader users; the sticky field-label column and CandidateRow headers already convey it visually.
__hints.compare.empty.awaited: EmptyState fact shown when a ward has zero filed/contesting candidates to compare yet and app_settings.notification_date is not set (PRD §5.3, design-system §7.12) — a 200, not a broken table.
__hints.compare.empty.fact: EmptyState fact shown when a ward has zero filed/contesting candidates to compare yet and app_settings.notification_date IS set; {date} is the raw stored date string, same convention as ward.candidates.empty.fact.
__hints.compare.links.backToWard: Link back to /ward/{id} shown inside the comparison page's empty state.
__hints.common.opensInNewTab: aria-label on the external-link glyph next to a guided link-out button (design-system.md §7.13) — the glyph itself, not visually-hidden companion text.
__hints.common.officialLinkPending: Visible note shown next to a guided link-out button whose official URL has not been filled into app_settings yet (dependency register §4.8) — never invent a URL, show this instead.
__hints.checkRegistration.linkOut.label: Button label for the guided link-out to the official EC/CEO Karnataka electoral roll lookup on /check-registration.
__hints.voterId.form6.label: Button label for the guided link-out to the official Form 6 (new voter enrolment) process on /voting-guide/voter-id.
__hints.voterId.form8.label: Button label for the guided link-out to the official Form 8 (update/transfer) process on /voting-guide/voter-id.
__hints.findBooth.form.label: Label on the /voting-guide/find-booth address input, same pattern as home.form.label.
__hints.findBooth.form.submit: Submit button label on the /voting-guide/find-booth address form.
__hints.findBooth.result.boothLabel: aria-label on the rendered polling-booth result (name + address) on /voting-guide/find-booth.
__hints.findBooth.result.noBoothData: Shown when booth-level data has not been loaded yet for the resolved ward (or at all) — PRD §5.10's guided link-out state, points the visitor at the official booth finder further down the page.
__hints.findBooth.result.unavailable: Shown when the address lookup itself failed/was ambiguous/hit budget (no pincode fallback for booths, unlike the ward finder).
__hints.findBooth.linkOut.heading: Heading above the always-visible guided link-out to the official EC booth finder on /voting-guide/find-booth — the no-JS fallback (PRD §5.10).
__hints.findBooth.linkOut.label: Button label for the guided link-out to the official EC/CEO Karnataka polling booth finder.
__hints.votingGuide.steps.heading: Heading above the 6-step deep-link checklist on /voting-guide (PRD §5.17) — distinct from the in-content '## What do I need to do before election day?' prose heading.
__hints.votingGuide.steps.findWard: Step 3 deep-link label on /voting-guide, to '/' (the ward finder).
__hints.votingGuide.steps.readCandidates: Step 4 deep-link label on /voting-guide — candidate report cards are per-ward, so this also links to '/' (find your ward first).
__hints.common.notDeclared: Shown in place of a candidate field the candidate has not filled in — neutral, not accusatory.
__hints.common.translationPending: Subtle indicator (FieldRow, design-system: no alarm styling) shown next to a curator-authored field when it's being displayed in the OTHER language and the machine translation hasn't landed yet (PRD §8, architecture §9) — the value shown is the AUTHORED-language text, not a translation.
__hints.common.source.curator: Distinguish from official/affidavit sources — this is our editorial team's compiled context, not a government document.
__hints.common.flagError: Action label on the button citizens tap to report incorrect info on any field.
__hints.common.deadlineBanner.defaultLabel: Default label on the statutory-deadline countdown banner (design-system.md §7.6); a specific page may override with its own label key, e.g. voter-registration roll closure.
__hints.common.deadlineBanner.daysLeft: The countdown figure suffix, e.g. '12 days left' — {days} is a plain integer, not localized-plural-aware yet (MVP).
__hints.common.badge.flagPending: Status chip on a citizen's own flagged-correction list (/account/submissions) while a curator has not yet acted on it.
__hints.common.badge.flagAccepted: Status chip when a curator accepted and published the citizen's flagged correction.
__hints.common.badge.flagRejected: Status chip when a curator reviewed and rejected the citizen's flagged correction.
__hints.common.badge.wardReady: Curator/admin chip: this ward's report cards meet the publish-readiness bar.
__hints.common.badge.wardHeld: Curator/admin chip: this ward is missing required data and is held back from readiness — a work state, not an error (design-system.md §7.13).
__hints.home.meta.title: Browser tab title / <title> for the Home page ('/', '/kn/').
__hints.home.hero.heading: Home page H1 above the ward search form.
__hints.home.form.label: Label on the Home ward-search input, which accepts either a free-text address or a 6-digit pincode.
__hints.home.form.helper: Helper text under the address input clarifying the pincode fallback path (PRD §5.1).
__hints.home.result.shortlistHeading: Shown above the list of ward links when a pincode maps to more than one ward.
__hints.home.result.outOfCoverage: Shown when an address or pincode resolves outside the GBA area — an explicit answer, not an error (PRD §5.1).
__hints.home.result.usePincode: Shown when address geocoding was ambiguous, budget-exhausted, or failed and the citizen is nudged to the pincode fallback instead.
__hints.home.election.awaited: Home page election-status banner before the notification date is set in app_settings — one Home page, no separate pre-notification variant (IA §3.1).
__hints.home.election.status: Home page election-status line once both notification_date and election_date are set in app_settings; {notificationDate} and {electionDate} are the raw stored date strings.
__hints.home.shortcuts.checkRegistration.descrip: Subtext under the Home page shortcut card linking to /check-registration.
__hints.ward.meta.title: Browser tab title for the ward result page (/ward/{id}); {wardName} is the ward's name in the page's own language, {wardId} is the plain numeric wards.id.
__hints.ward.meta.description: Meta description for the ward result page.
__hints.ward.heading.wardNumber: Small label next to the ward name on the ward result page H1, e.g. 'Ward 57' — {wardId} is the plain numeric wards.id, not translated/formatted further.
__hints.ward.corporation.north: Display label for the 'north' corporation enum value (IA §3.2: ward name + number + corporation, N/S/E/W/Central) — kept as a short direction word, not the full 'Bengaluru North' corporation name.
__hints.ward.corporation.south: Display label for the 'south' corporation enum value — see ward.corporation.north.
__hints.ward.corporation.east: Display label for the 'east' corporation enum value — see ward.corporation.north.
__hints.ward.corporation.west: Display label for the 'west' corporation enum value — see ward.corporation.north.
__hints.ward.corporation.central: Display label for the 'central' corporation enum value — see ward.corporation.north.
__hints.ward.map.label: aria-label on the ward result page's boundary-map section.
__hints.ward.map.fallback: Static text inside the WardMap island's container, shown until (or unless) MapLibre replaces it — the no-JS/JS-failure fallback for design-system.md §8's boundary map.
__hints.ward.links.label: aria-label on the ward result page's nav of links to the candidates/issues/voting-guide subpages.
__hints.ward.links.candidates: Link label on the ward result page to /ward/{id}/candidates (IA §3.3).
__hints.ward.links.issues: Link label on the ward result page to /ward/{id}/issues (IA §3.6).
__hints.ward.notFound.fact: Defensive-only fallback fact (EmptyState) if Ward.astro is ever reached with an id its own route twin failed to 404 for — not a path any test exercises (the twins 404 first).
__hints.ward.issues.meta.title: Browser tab title for the ward issues page (/ward/{id}/issues); {wardName} is the ward's name in the page's own language.
__hints.ward.issues.meta.description: Meta description for the ward issues page.
__hints.ward.issues.subheading: Small label under the ward name on the ward issues page H1, matching the ward result page's corporation-label treatment.
__hints.ward.issues.empty.fact: EmptyState fact when a ward has no curator-defined issues yet (PRD §13.1 Phase 1 — issues/voting ship before candidate data).
__hints.ward.issues.empty.nextStep: EmptyState next-step text paired with ward.issues.empty.fact.
__hints.ward.issues.list.label: aria-label on the ward issues page's list of curator-defined issues.
__hints.ward.issues.item.stancesLabel: aria-label on the list of candidate stances shown under each issue (IA §3.6) — omitted when an issue has no stances yet.
__hints.ward.issues.results.label: aria-label on the public issue-vote results section (IssueBars) — ranked order with percentage shares only, no raw counts (PRD §5.5).
__hints.ward.issues.results.heading: Visible heading above the public issue-vote results (IssueBars).
__hints.legal.courtesyNote: Courtesy-translation notice (PRD §5.16) shown ONLY on the Kannada /terms and /privacy pages, never on English or /about — states the English version of the legal page is the legally controlling text and the Kannada version is a courtesy translation. Rendered as a notice Banner at the top of the page.
__hints.error.notFound.title: Task 23 — <title> and EmptyState 'fact' on the friendly 404 page (/404, /kn/404). Also used as the page's meta description alongside error.notFound.body, matching the ward.notFound.fact pattern.
__hints.error.notFound.body: EmptyState 'next-step' text on the 404 page, under error.notFound.title.
__hints.error.serverError.title: Task 23 — <title> and EmptyState 'fact' on the generic 500 error page (/500) — this page must render with zero DB/settings dependency, so keep this static and short.
__hints.error.serverError.body: EmptyState 'next-step' text on the 500 page, under error.serverError.title.
__hints.error.backHome: Link label back to '/' from both the 404 and 500 pages.
__hints.auth.meta.title: Browser tab title for /login (Task 27) — the noindex no-JS fallback for the Register/Login modal (IA §7.1).
__hints.auth.modal.title: Fixed <h2> title of the Register/Login modal AND the <h1> of its /login fallback page — same title across all three steps (design-system.md §7.9: modal title in --text-2xl).
__hints.auth.modal.close: aria-label on the Register/Login modal's explicit Close button (design-system.md §7.9).
__hints.auth.step1.contactLabel: Label on the SINGLE contact input (email or WhatsApp number) — the channel is inferred from the value (contains '@' -> email), not a separate toggle (design-system.md §7.9: 'single input per step').
__hints.auth.step1.helper: Helper text under the contact input on step 1.
__hints.auth.step1.submit: Submit button label on step 1 (send the OTP code).
__hints.auth.step1.sent: Shown once the code has been requested — deliberately generic (PRD §10 non-disclosure: never reveals whether the contact was already registered).
__hints.auth.step1.whatsappNudge: Shown when requestOtp returns 'send_failed' for a WhatsApp destination (WhatsApp isn't onboarded yet, PRD §10) — nudges the visitor to use email instead, the baseline delivery channel.
__hints.auth.step1.error.required: Client/server validation message when the contact field is submitted empty.
__hints.auth.step2.codeLabel: Label on the ONE 6-digit OTP input (inputmode=numeric, autocomplete=one-time-code) — never six separate boxes (design-system.md §7.9).
__hints.auth.step2.submit: Submit button label on step 2 (verify the code).
__hints.auth.step2.backLabel: Link/button on step 2 to go back and re-enter the contact (request a fresh code with a different email/WhatsApp number).
__hints.auth.step2.error.invalid: Shown when verifyOtp/resolveOrRegister returns reason:'invalid' (wrong code, attempts remaining).
__hints.auth.step2.error.locked: Shown when verifyOtp/resolveOrRegister returns reason:'locked' (5 wrong attempts).
__hints.auth.step2.error.account_banned: Shown when resolveOrRegister finds the contact (existing user) but the user's status is 'banned' or 'erased' (Task 45 review: fix to reject banned/erased users at login).
__hints.auth.step3.wardLabel: Label on step 3's ward number input (shown read-only, pre-filled, when opened from a ward page's register-for-updates slot — otherwise an editable numeric input; IA §7.1).
__hints.auth.step3.languageLabel: Label on step 3's language select (en/kn) — the registered user's saved notification-language preference (PRD §8).
__hints.auth.step3.languageOption.en: Option label for English in the step 3 language select.
__hints.auth.step3.languageOption.kn: Option label for Kannada in the step 3 language select — rendered in the Kannada script itself regardless of the surrounding page's language, same convention as AppBar's language toggle.
__hints.auth.step3.consentSentence: The consent sentence PRD §10 requires: what registering signs the citizen up for, plus the 18+ assertion, shown alongside (not inside) the /terms and /privacy links — completing registration IS the consent act, there is no separate checkbox for this sentence.
__hints.auth.step3.termsLink: Link text to /terms from the step 3 consent sentence.
__hints.auth.step3.privacyLink: Link text to /privacy from the step 3 consent sentence.
__hints.auth.step3.futureTools: Label on the ONE optional, unchecked-by-default 'future civic tools' checkbox (PRD §10) — distinct from the consent sentence above, which is not a checkbox.
__hints.auth.step3.submit: Submit button label on step 3 (complete registration).
__hints.auth.step3.codeExpired: Shown on /login when the OTP code expires in the gap between step 2 and step 3 (the code is reused across those two steps) — sends the visitor back to step 1 to request a new one.
__hints.auth.step3.error.ward: Validation message when the /login step 3 ward-number field is missing/not a whole number (the JS modal's read-only pre-filled ward never hits this path).
__hints.account.ward.current: Shown on /account above the ward-change form — {ward} is the ward's own bilingual name (already resolved to the current UI language before interpolation), not a ward number.
__hints.account.ward.changeHelper: Helper text under the /account ward-change field warning that submitting a DIFFERENT ward number retires the citizen's current top-3 issue votes (PRD §5.5) — they are not lost, just no longer counted, and can be re-cast in the new ward.
__hints.account.contact.addLabel: Label on the SINGLE new-contact input on /account (email or WhatsApp number) — same single-input-per-step pattern as auth.step1.contactLabel.
__hints.account.contact.step1.sent: Shown on /account once a new contact's OTP code has been requested, right above the code-entry form — deliberately generic, mirrors auth.step1.sent.
__hints.account.contact.error.taken: Shown on /account step 2 when the verified new contact's OTP is correct but the contact already belongs to a DIFFERENT account (PRD §10 one-account-per-contact) — the new contact is not attached.
__hints.account.submissions.reasonLabel: Short label prefixed before the curator's resolutionReason text on /account/submissions, e.g. 'Reason: <text>' — only shown once a flag is accepted/rejected (PRD §6.3).
__hints.flag.modal.title: Fixed <h2> title of the Flag misinformation modal (Task 32, IA §7.2, design-system.md §7.9).
__hints.flag.modal.close: aria-label on the Flag modal's explicit Close button.
__hints.flag.modal.targetLabel: aria-labelledby text above the field/claim picker (radiogroup) — 'which field/claim is wrong', built client-side from the targets the opening button carried.
__hints.flag.modal.detailLabel: Label on the required free-text detail textarea — what specifically is wrong with the selected field/claim.
__hints.flag.modal.sourceLabel: Label on the optional source-URL input backing a citizen's flagged correction.
__hints.flag.modal.suggestedLabel: Label on the optional suggested-value input — what the citizen believes the correct value is.
__hints.flag.modal.submit: Submit button label on the Flag modal.
__hints.flag.modal.rateLimited: Shown inline when POST /api/flags returns 429 (Task 31's per-day flag budget) — try again later, not a queued/degraded submit.
__hints.flag.modal.sourceInvalid: Shown on the source-URL field when POST /api/flags returns 400 for a non-http(s) sourceUrl (architecture §13).
__hints.flag.modal.genericError: Shown on any other unexpected non-2xx/network failure submitting a flag.
__hints.flag.modal.success: Success toast text shown after a flag is accepted (200) and the modal closes (PRD §6.1 step 2 — lands in the ward's review queue).
__hints.ward.flag.wardFieldLabel: The single ward_field flag target's picker label on the ward result page (Ward.astro) — kept minimal/generic since the page shows the ward's name/number/corporation as one block.
__hints.vote.modal.title: Fixed <h2> title of the Cast issue vote modal (Task 33, IA §3.6/§7, design-system.md §7.9).
__hints.vote.modal.close: aria-label on the Vote modal's explicit Close button.
__hints.vote.modal.instructions: Short instruction line above the checkbox list explaining the up-to-three cap.
__hints.vote.modal.issuesLabel: aria-label on the checkbox group listing this ward's current issues.
__hints.vote.modal.submitTemplate: Submit button label template, e.g. 'Vote (2 of 3 selected)' (design-system.md §7.9) — {n} is replaced CLIENT-SIDE (src/islands/VoteModal.ts), not through the usual t()-time interpolation, since the count changes live as checkboxes are toggled.
__hints.vote.modal.rateLimited: Shown inline when PUT /api/issue-votes returns 429 (the per-day re-cast budget, src/lib/rate-limit.ts) — try again later, not a queued/degraded submit.
__hints.vote.modal.genericError: Shown on any other unexpected non-2xx/network failure casting a vote.
__hints.vote.modal.success: Success toast text shown after a vote is cast/re-cast (200) and the modal closes.
__hints.vote.modal.homeWardOnlyTemplate: Shown INSTEAD of the checkbox form when the authed visitor's home ward isn't the ward this modal was opened against (PRD §5.5 — voting is home-ward-only). {wardId} is replaced CLIENT-SIDE with their actual home ward's id (a number — the modal has no ward-name lookup of its own).
__hints.vote.modal.changeHomeWard: Link label (to /account) paired with vote.modal.homeWardOnlyTemplate, letting the visitor go change their registered home ward.
__hints.curator.dashboard.queueCount: Pending-flag count on the curator dashboard's review-queue card (Task 34, IA §5.1) — {count} is a plain integer.
__hints.curator.dashboard.signOff.clearedNote: Shown next to a ward on the awaiting-sign-off list whose sign-off was CLEARED by a later candidate-set change (PRD §9.1) — these are called out first because a curator who doesn't know a ward is held won't sign it off.
__hints.curator.queueItem.heading: H1 on the flag-review page (/curator/queue/{id}), Task 34, IA §5.3 — {ward} is the ward's name in the curator's own saved UI language.
__hints.curator.queueItem.resolutionReason: Shown once a flag is accepted/rejected, echoing the curator's own reason back on this same page — {reason} is the resolutionReason column's free text.
__hints.curator.queueItem.currentValue.candidate: Shown above the current-value block for a candidate_field target — {name} is the candidate's own English name (candidate_fields has no per-candidate Kannada name guarantee).
__hints.curator.queueItem.currentValue.field: Shown above the current-value block — {field} is the raw candidate_fields.field_key (e.g. 'cases'), not translated.
__hints.curator.queueItem.accept.confirm: Label on the REQUIRED confirmation checkbox above the Accept & publish button (design-system.md §7.13: 'confirmation dialogs stating scope') — {path} is a literal URL path like '/ward/57' and must NOT be translated or altered.
__hints.curator.queueItem.accept.unsupportedNote: Shown in place of the Accept form for a ward_field/ward_issue flag target — that publish path lands in a later task (Task 39); only Reject is available for these today.
__hints.curator.queueItem.error.acceptUnsupporte: Defensive validation error if an Accept POST is somehow submitted for a ward_field/ward_issue target — the UI already hides that form (curator.queueItem.accept.unsupportedNote), this is belt-and-suspenders.
__hints.curator.queueItem.alreadyResolved: Shown when a curator submits accept/reject on a flag item another covering curator (queues are per-ward, scopes may overlap, PRD §6.1) already resolved a moment earlier — a friendly notice, not an error page.
__hints.curator.candidateEdit.heading: H1 on the candidate editor (/curator/candidate/{id}), Task 36, IA §5.4 — {name} is the candidate's own English name.
__hints.curator.candidateEdit.new.heading: H1 on the new-candidate page (/curator/candidate/new?ward={id}) — {ward} is the ward's name in the curator's own saved UI language.
__hints.curator.candidateEdit.wardLine: Small ward-name line under the H1 on both the edit and new-candidate pages — {ward} is the ward's name in the curator's own saved UI language.
__hints.curator.candidateEdit.core.statusWarning: Static (always-visible, no-JS) warning next to the lifecycle status <select> — a STATUS transition clears the ward's sign-off (architecture §6, PRD §9.1); this is not conditional on JS detecting a change.
__hints.curator.candidateEdit.affidavit.note: Placeholder note where the affidavit upload + AI-extraction UI (Task 37) will mount — this task deliberately does not implement extraction.
__hints.curator.candidateEdit.newsLinks.suggeste: Badge shown next to an auto-suggested (origin 'auto', status 'suggested') news link on the curator editor — signals the link is NOT yet visible to the public until a curator approves it (PRD §5.2: nothing publishes unapproved).
__hints.curator.candidateEdit.newsLinks.existing: Sub-heading above the list of a candidate's news links (both suggested and approved) on the curator editor.
__hints.curator.candidateEdit.newsLinks.addHeadi: Sub-heading above the 'add a link' form on the curator editor's news-links panel.
__hints.curator.wardEdit.readiness.noCandidates: Shown INSTEAD of a gap list when the ward has zero filed/contesting candidates (Task 39, PRD §9.1) — distinct from 'candidates exist but all complete', which shows no gap list at all because there IS nothing to list.
__hints.curator.wardEdit.readiness.gapItem: One line per candidate with a data gap on the readiness panel (design-system.md §7.13, sun-tint 'held' state — never red) — {name} is the candidate's English name, {fields} is their missing/sourceless field keys joined with ', ' (e.g. 'cases, assets').
__hints.curator.wardEdit.signOff.signedOffLine: Shown when the ward currently has a live sign-off — {at} is an ISO timestamp, {by} is the signing curator/admin's email (or their numeric user id if no email is on file).
__hints.curator.wardEdit.signOff.clearedByChange: Shown when the ward WAS signed off but a later candidate-set change (a status transition or a new filing, PRD §9.1) cleared it — distinct wording from 'not yet signed off' so a curator knows this ward needs a FRESH look, not a first one.
__hints.curator.wardIssuesEdit.remove.confirmLab: Label on the REQUIRED confirmation checkbox next to each issue's Remove button (design-system.md §7.13: destructive actions get a confirmation stating scope) — removing an issue cascades away its own vote selections; PRD §5.5 requires this to be understood before it happens.
__hints.admin.console.overview.pendingEoi: Overview count on the admin console home (/admin, Task 44, IA §6.1) — the number of eoi_submissions rows still in status 'new', i.e. awaiting an admin's accept/decline decision.
__hints.admin.roles.grant.lookupLabel: Label on the admin Roles page's 'grant a role' text input (/admin/roles, Task 44, IA §6.2) — accepts EITHER a numeric user id OR an email address; src/lib/admin.ts's findUserIdByLookup tries both.
__hints.admin.roles.grant.confirmLabel: Label on the REQUIRED confirmation checkbox above the grant-role submit button (design-system.md §7.13: destructive/publish actions get a confirmation stating scope) — the granted role (curator or admin, whichever the adjacent <select> has chosen) takes effect immediately, PRD §14 (no approval gate).
__hints.admin.roles.list.colWards: Table column header on the admin Roles page's curator/admin overview (Task 44, IA §6.2) — shows a ward count + names for a curator, or 'City-wide' for an admin (city-wide access is never a stored per-ward set, PRD §7/§10).
__hints.admin.roles.manage.revoke.confirmLabel: Label on the REQUIRED confirmation checkbox next to each user's Revoke-role button (design-system.md §7.13) — {email} is that user's own email (or their numeric id if none is on file); revoking ALSO clears every curator_scopes row for them (PRD §10 — a demoted curator has no scope).
__hints.admin.roles.manage.scope.wardIdsHelper: Helper text under the per-curator ward-ids text field on the admin Roles page (Task 44, IA §6.2, PRD §10) — clarifies that submitting REPLACES the full stored scope (src/lib/admin.ts's setCuratorScope), it does not merge with what's currently saved (the zone shortcut below is the one thing that DOES merge, into this same field's value, at save time).
__hints.admin.roles.manage.scope.zoneLabel: Label on the per-curator zone-shortcut <select> (PRD §10, IA §6.2: 'a zone shortcut that expands to that zone's wards') — picking a zone here does not replace the typed ward-ids field; the route twin unions the zone's ward ids into it at save time. There is no stored 'zone scope' concept — only the resulting per-ward curator_scopes rows persist.
__hints.admin.roles.manage.scope.confirmLabel: Label on the REQUIRED confirmation checkbox above the per-curator Save-scope button (design-system.md §7.13) — {email} is that user's own email (or their numeric id if none is on file); saving REPLACES their entire ward scope (uncapped, PRD §14), not an incremental add.
__hints.admin.roles.error.invalidWardId: Shown when the scope form's ward-ids text (plus any zone-expanded ids) includes an id that isn't a real wards.id — src/lib/admin.ts's setCuratorScope validates every id before writing anything, so this rejection leaves the previously-saved scope completely untouched.
__hints.admin.roles.error.cannotRevokeSelf: Lockout-prevention guard (Task 44 review) — shown when an admin submits revoke, or a grant that would demote an admin to curator, targeting their OWN user id. Never a 500: src/lib/admin.ts's assertNotSelfOrLastAdmin rejects before any write, so the caller stays admin. Another admin must perform the demotion instead.
__hints.admin.roles.error.cannotRevokeLastAdmin: Lockout-prevention guard (Task 44 review) — shown when a revoke, or a grant that would demote an admin to curator, targets the last remaining ACTIVE admin (src/lib/admin.ts's assertNotSelfOrLastAdmin counts role='admin' AND status='active' rows inside the same transaction). Grant another admin the admin role first, then the original demotion can proceed.
__hints.admin.users.search.label: Label on the admin Manage-users search box (/admin/users, Task 45, IA §6.3) — a plain GET query (?q=), not a POST, so results are a bookmarkable/shareable URL. Matches a bare integer against the user id, and anything typed against BOTH email and phone as a case-insensitive substring (src/lib/erasure.ts's searchUsers) — a purely-numeric query still checks phone substrings too, since a partial phone number is also all digits.
__hints.admin.users.results.colFlags: Table column header on the admin Manage-users results table (Task 45, IA §6.3 'view submission history') — shows a COUNT of flag_submissions rows for that user id, not the submissions themselves; the flags/audit tooling elsewhere is where the full detail lives.
__hints.admin.users.results.noContact: Shown in the Contact column for a row whose status is 'erased' — email and phone are both null after the DPDP erasure routine (src/lib/erasure.ts's eraseUser), by design (architecture.md §7): there is no contact left to show.
__hints.admin.users.ban.reasonLabel: Optional free-text field on the Ban form (Task 45, IA §6.3 'deactivate/ban accounts') — folded into the ban_user audit row's newValue as a moderation NOTE (e.g. 'spam', 'abusive comments'), never the target user's own PII (architecture §13 'no PII in audit').
__hints.admin.users.ban.confirmLabel: Label on the REQUIRED confirmation checkbox above the per-user Ban button (design-system.md §7.13: destructive actions get a confirmation stating scope) — {user} is that user's email, or their numeric id if erased/no contact on file; banning also deletes every one of their sessions rows immediately (src/lib/erasure.ts's banUser), signing them out everywhere.
__hints.admin.users.reactivate.confirmLabel: Label on the REQUIRED confirmation checkbox above the per-user Reactivate button — {user} is that user's email, or their numeric id; only shown for a 'banned' row (an 'erased' row can never be reactivated, src/lib/erasure.ts's reactivateUser rejects it).
__hints.admin.users.erase.heading: Section heading over the per-user DPDP erasure form (Task 45, PRD §5.16, architecture.md §7) — deliberately visually separated from Ban/Reactivate as the one IRREVERSIBLE action on this page.
__hints.admin.users.erase.warning: Banner text shown above the Erase form's confirmation checkbox, stating in plain language exactly what architecture.md §7's erasure routine does — deletes contact/session/consent data, keeps votes/flags/audit as an anonymous, untraceable record — so an admin confirms with full knowledge of the irreversible consequence, not just a generic 'are you sure'. {user} is that user's email, or their numeric id if already erased-adjacent.
__hints.admin.users.erase.confirmLabel: Label on the REQUIRED confirmation checkbox above the per-user Erase button (design-system.md §7.13, escalated for an IRREVERSIBLE action per PRD §5.16) — {user} is that user's email, or their numeric id; src/lib/erasure.ts's eraseUser runs immediately on submit, in one transaction, with no undo.
__hints.admin.users.error.cannotBanErased: Shown when an admin attempts to Ban a row whose status is already 'erased' — src/lib/erasure.ts's banUser rejects this (erasure is the terminal state; an opaque tombstone is never moved back to 'banned').
__hints.admin.users.error.cannotReactivateErased: Shown when an admin attempts to Reactivate a row whose status is 'erased' — src/lib/erasure.ts's reactivateUser rejects this; only a 'banned' account can be restored to 'active'.
__hints.admin.partners.add.slugLabel: Label on the add-partner form's slug field (/admin/partners, Task 46, IA §6.4) — this exact slug is used verbatim in the public /partner/{slug} kit page URL (Task 48) and in the ?src={slug} attribution query param, so it must be URL-safe (src/lib/partners.ts's isValidPartnerSlug).
__hints.admin.partners.add.wardIdsLabel: Comma-separated ward-id text field on the add-partner form — the partner's INITIAL coverage set (partner_wards rows); may be left blank for a partner with no ward coverage yet.
__hints.admin.partners.add.confirmLabel: Label on the REQUIRED confirmation checkbox above the add-partner submit button (design-system.md §7.13) — a created partner is live immediately (its slug/kit page and ?src= attribution start working right away, PRD §5.12).
__hints.admin.partners.edit.confirmLabel: Label on the REQUIRED confirmation checkbox on each partner's edit form — {slug} is that partner's slug; submitting REPLACES the partner's entire ward-coverage set (src/lib/partners.ts's updatePartner), it does not merge with the existing one.
__hints.admin.partners.edit.wardIdsLabel: Helper label on the per-partner ward-coverage text field (Task 46, IA §6.4) — clarifies that submitting REPLACES the full stored coverage set, same replace-not-merge convention as admin.roles.manage.scope.wardIdsHelper.
__hints.admin.partners.coverage.summary: One-line coverage-matrix summary on the admin Partners page (Task 46, IA §6.4) — {covered} and {total} are both plain integer counts of wards (out of the full 369-ward city) with at least one partner covering them.
__hints.admin.partners.coverage.uncoveredHeading: Heading over the UNCOVERED-wards work queue (Task 46, IA §6.4) — the early-warning signal for central-Bengaluru distribution skew: wards with zero partner_wards rows, i.e. no distribution partner reaching them yet.
__hints.admin.partners.coverage.uncoveredCount: Count line above the uncovered-wards list — {count} is the number of wards with no partner covering them (src/lib/partners.ts's partnerCoverage `uncovered` array length).
__hints.admin.partners.held.heading: Heading over the held-wards table (Task 46, IA §6.4, PRD §9.1) — wards that src/lib/readiness.ts's isWardReadyForComms would currently hold BACK from candidate-comms sends (incomplete data, never signed off, or signed-off-then-cleared by a later candidate-set change); a held ward is a send that won't go out until fixed or overridden.
__hints.admin.partners.held.reason.incomplete: Held-ward reason label — the ward's candidates have data gaps per src/lib/readiness.ts's computeReadiness (missing name/party/cases/assets/education, or a populated field with no source).
__hints.admin.partners.held.reason.unsigned: Held-ward reason label — the ward's data is complete but no curator/admin has ever signed it off (src/lib/readiness.ts's signOffWard has never been called for this ward).
__hints.admin.partners.held.reason.cleared: Held-ward reason label — the ward WAS signed off, but a later candidate-set change (a status transition or new filing) cleared that sign-off (src/lib/readiness.ts's wasClearedByChange) and it has not been re-signed since.
__hints.admin.partners.held.overriddenLabel: Badge text shown next to an already-overridden held ward (Task 46, PRD §9.1) — an admin has already set commsHoldOverride=true for it, so src/lib/readiness.ts's isWardReadyForComms treats its send as released despite the underlying hold reason still showing for transparency.
__hints.admin.partners.held.override.confirmLabe: Label on the REQUIRED confirmation checkbox next to each held ward's Override button (design-system.md §7.13) — {ward} is that ward's name; this sets a WARD-LEVEL commsHoldOverride flag (src/lib/partners.ts's overrideCommsHold) that releases every future candidate-comms send for the ward, not a one-time pass — PRD §9.1's admin oversight valve.
__hints.admin.partners.eoi.awareness.heading: Sub-heading over the 'awareness' path of the EOI queue (Task 46, IA §6.4, PRD §5.13) — accepting one of these PROVISIONS a partner (slug + /partner/{slug} kit page, Task 48).
__hints.admin.partners.eoi.curation.heading: Sub-heading over the 'curation' path of the EOI queue — accepting one of these does NOT grant the curator role by itself (PRD §5.13: no self-activation); see admin.partners.eoi.accept.curation.note.
__hints.admin.partners.eoi.colWards: Table column header on the EOI queue — the free-text wards-of-interest field the applicant typed on the public EOI form (eoi_submissions.wardsText), shown as-is, not parsed into real ward ids.
__hints.admin.partners.eoi.accept.awareness.slug: Label on the awareness-path accept form's slug field — the NEW partner's slug (src/lib/partners.ts's acceptEoiAwareness calls createPartner with this), same URL-safety rule as admin.partners.add.slugLabel.
__hints.admin.partners.eoi.accept.awareness.conf: Label on the REQUIRED confirmation checkbox on the awareness-path accept form (design-system.md §7.13) — accepting immediately creates a live partner (slug + kit page), same immediate-effect convention as admin.partners.add.confirmLabel.
__hints.admin.partners.eoi.accept.curation.confi: Label on the REQUIRED confirmation checkbox on the curation-path accept form — spells out explicitly that accepting here is NOT the same action as granting the curator role (PRD §5.13's no-self-activation rule; see admin.partners.eoi.accept.curation.note for the actual next step).
__hints.admin.partners.eoi.accept.curation.note: Static note shown next to the curation-path accept form (Task 46, IA §6.4: 'accepting curation -> hands off to /admin/roles') — accepting an EOI here only records admin intent; src/lib/partners.ts's acceptEoiCuration never touches users.role or curator_scopes, by design (PRD §5.13, no self-activation).
__hints.partnerKit.meta.title: Browser tab title on the unlisted partner kit page (/partner/{slug}, Task 48, IA §3.19) — {name} is the partner's own name (e.g. an RWA's name), not translated.
__hints.partnerKit.meta.description: Meta description for the partner kit page — {name} is the partner's own name, not translated.
__hints.partnerKit.heading: H1 on the partner kit page — {name} is the partner's own name (e.g. an RWA or civic org), not translated.
__hints.partnerKit.intro: Short intro paragraph under the H1 summarizing what the kit contains — tagged link, forward texts, poster, neutrality statement.
__hints.partnerKit.notFound.fact: EmptyState fact shown for an unknown partner slug — the real 404 status itself comes from the route twin (src/pages/partner/[slug].astro), same convention as candidate.notFound.fact.
__hints.partnerKit.taggedLink.heading: Heading over the partner's tagged link block (PRD §5.12 — /?src={slug}, persisted onto the user record at registration for attribution).
__hints.partnerKit.taggedLink.helper: One-line helper under the tagged-link heading explaining WHY it's tagged (attribution, not tracking of the individual).
__hints.partnerKit.copyButton: Label on every copy-to-clipboard button next to a readonly link/text field on this page (the tagged link, and each WhatsApp forward-text block) — a small progressive enhancement over select+copy, which always still works with zero JS.
__hints.partnerKit.copiedLabel: Momentary label a copy button switches to right after a successful navigator.clipboard.writeText(), also announced via the page's aria-live status region — reverts to partnerKit.copyButton's own label a couple seconds later (src/islands/CopyButton.ts).
__hints.partnerKit.langLabel.en: Label identifying the English-language copy of a WhatsApp forward text block — shown on BOTH the /partner/{slug} and /kn/partner/{slug} pages, since a partner may want to grab either language's text regardless of which page they're on (IA §3.19).
__hints.partnerKit.langLabel.kn: Label identifying the Kannada-language copy of a WhatsApp forward text block — same rationale as partnerKit.langLabel.en.
__hints.partnerKit.forward.sectionHeading: Section heading over the WhatsApp forward-text blocks (PRD §5.12) — 'the unit of distribution is a message pasted into an apartment WhatsApp group' (IA §3.19's own note).
__hints.partnerKit.forward.general.heading: Sub-heading over the general (non-first-time-voter) forward-text pair (EN + KN blocks underneath).
__hints.partnerKit.forward.general.body: The general WhatsApp forward message — a short 'find your GBA ward and who's standing' pitch plus the partner's own tagged link. {link} is that tagged link (/?src={slug}), interpolated verbatim — never translate or alter the URL itself.
__hints.partnerKit.forward.firstTimeVoter.headin: Sub-heading over the first-time-voter forward-text pair — PRD §5.12/§5.17: a variant aimed at first-time voters, linking the /voting-guide checklist.
__hints.partnerKit.forward.firstTimeVoter.body: The first-time-voter WhatsApp forward message — a short pitch aimed at someone voting for the first time (registration, voter ID, what to expect) plus the /voting-guide checklist link. {link} is that link, already localized to this text's OWN language (the EN block links the EN checklist, the KN block links the KN checklist) with the partner's ?src attribution — interpolated verbatim, never translate or alter the URL itself.
__hints.partnerKit.poster.heading: Heading over the poster block — a WhatsApp-sized poster image (design-system §11: the one page in this system where print styles matter).
__hints.partnerKit.poster.pendingNote: Placeholder note shown inside the poster block until the real poster asset is supplied (an 'INPUT NEEDED' asset, per the dependency-register convention used elsewhere — e.g. src/lib/settings.ts's roll_lookup_url note) — never a fabricated placeholder image standing in for a real poster.
__hints.partnerKit.neutrality.heading: Heading over the neutrality statement — PRD §5.12: 'the answer ships with the kit' for when a partner is accused of campaigning.
__hints.partnerKit.neutrality.statement: The neutrality statement itself (PRD §5.12) — states plainly that this is a neutral, non-partisan platform run by the Oorvani Foundation and that forwarding it is sharing voter information, not campaigning. This exact framing (operator name, 'non-partisan', 'not campaigning') should be preserved in translation, not paraphrased loosely.
